items.title: Cache key for embeddings
items.scenario: An embedding service caches results to skip duplicate API calls.
items.explanation: `key = (model, hash(text))` is a small tuple — immutable, hashable, perfect as a dict key.

items.title: Shape of a tensor
items.scenario: An embedding vector has a fixed dimensionality, e.g. 768 or 1536.
items.explanation: `np.zeros((768,)).shape == (768,)` — shape is naturally a tuple because each axis is a labeled, fixed slot.

items.title: Multiple return values
items.scenario: A parser needs to return both a title and a body from a document.
items.explanation: `return title, body` packs into a tuple; the caller unpacks with `title, body = parse(doc)`.

items.title: Coordinates and ranges
items.scenario: A chunker labels each chunk with `(start_offset, end_offset)` in the source document.
items.explanation: Each chunk has exactly two integers in fixed positions — a tuple captures that intent.
